video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at a beer hall in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people
video:video.video:description: A huge fire tore through a beer hall in Bangkok overnight into Monday, killing at least 27 people and injuring dozens before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

video:video.video:title: Fire in Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people
video:video.video:description: A fire which engulfed a beer hall in Bangkok overnight killed at least 27 people and injured dozens more. Firefighters brought the blaze under control early Monday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 13
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, July 13th: U.S. launches new strikes in Iran; U.S. and Iran claim control of Strait of Hormuz; Bangkok beer hall fire kills dozens; Aorta tear blamed for Senator Lindsey Graham’s death.

video:video.video:title: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78
video:video.video:description: Actor Sam Neill's death was “sudden and unexpected,” a family statement said, adding that he “remained cancer free” when he died. A cause of death wasn’t specified.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya reveals who told her she'd play playing Athena
video:video.video:description: Zendaya just revealed the surprising way she found out she'd landed the role of Athena in Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey"

video:video.video:title: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, one of President Donald Trump’s closest allies in Congress who traveled the globe to advocate for a more aggressive U.S. foreign policy, died after a tear in his aorta, according to a preliminary medical examiner finding shared by his office.

video:video.video:title: 20 days on, Venezuelans still trying to recover bodies of their relatives killed in earthquakes
video:video.video:description: 20 days on, Venezuelans still trying to recover bodies of their relatives killed in earthquakes

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least five, Palestinian health officials say
video:video.video:description: Israeli strike in Gaza on Sunday killed at least five Palestinians and wounded over a dozen others, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Footage from outside Bangkok music bar shows deadly fire
video:video.video:description: A huge fire tore through a music bar in Bangkok overnight into Monday, killing at least 27 people and injuring dozens.

video:video.video:title: Video shows huge fire at a Bangkok beer hall that killed 27
video:video.video:description: Authorities are still investigating the cause of the fire, but Bangkok Gov. Chadchart Sittipunt said investigators would examine the ceiling materials and whether any emergency exits may have been obstructed, potentially making it more difficult for people to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Coalition of the Willing meeting joined by Zelenskyy opens in Paris
video:video.video:description: “Our goal is to build a shared ballistic missile defense capability for Europe,” the 10 nations said in a statement in Paris at talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

video:video.video:title: US Senator Lindsey Graham died after aorta tear, medical examiner says
video:video.video:description: The tear in the inner wall of the aorta, called an aortic dissection, was related to the hardening of Graham's arteries.

video:video.video:title: US military uses drone boats in a first-of-its-kind attack
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command says it used drone ships to hit an Iranian ship maintenance facility and submarine, calling it first.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland delayed 'Spider-Man' to be in 'The Odyssey'
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland says he was "nervous" asking Sony to push production for "Spider-Man: Brand New Day" so he could star in "The Odyssey."

video:video.video:title: Man who killed Dartmouth professors at 17 to get a chance at parole in about 20 years
video:video.video:description: A Vermont man who was 17 when he and a friend killed a pair of married Dartmouth College professors 25 years ago will have a chance at parole in about 20 years, when he reaches the age of one of his victims, a judge ruled Monday.

video:video.video:title: Moscow to 'respond in kind' to Ukraine with more powerful strikes, Putin says
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin on Monday said Moscow's response to Ukrainian attacks would always be "mirror-like" but "several times more powerful", he said as he toured an exhibition organised by the pro-Kremlin coalition All-Russia People's Front.

video:video.video:title: Chinese and Russian ships join naval drills in Yellow Sea
video:video.video:description: According to Russia’s Defense Ministry, ships from the two countries spent several days conducting joint drills, including live-fire exercises using artillery and machine-gun systems to repel a simulated attack by unmanned aerial vehicles and unmanned surface vessels.

video:video.video:title: Anya Taylor-Joy's World Cup dilemma
video:video.video:description: British-Argentine actor Anya Taylor-Joy says the World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina has thrown her allegiances into chaos.

video:video.video:title: Haaland brings back stuffed 'Whiskey Raccoon' as Norway squad arrive home
video:video.video:description: Erling Haaland brought an unusual souvenir back to Norway with him — a taxidermied raccoon which appeared to be posed drinking from a bottle.

video:video.video:title: Feds hand over evidence in Renee Good and Alex Pretti killings to Minnesota prosecutors
video:video.video:description: Minnesota prosecutors announced Monday that they have obtained key evidence in their ongoing investigations into the fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti during protests against a federal immigration enforcement crackdown in the state earlier this year.

video:video.video:title: Iran releases video said to show latest strikes against US bases in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The U.S. launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday, hours after President Donald Trump vowed to reinstate an American blockade of Iranian ports and charge ships for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Iran releases video said to show missiles being launched against US bases in Middle East
video:video.video:description: Iran's military on Tuesday released footage said to show missiles being launched towards U.S. bases in the region.

video:video.video:title: Italian region defies US pressure to end Cuban doctor program
video:video.video:description: Italy’s southern Calabria region is the rare place in Europe where Cuba sends medical professionals under a long-running program that the United States wants to stamp out.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle to stop forest blaze near Paris from erupting again
video:video.video:description: Fires have consumed nearly 2,000 hectares and forced the evacuation of about 1,000 people. Bigger fires have been ravaging areas of southern France, but the Fontainebleau fire is exceptionally close to the densely populated region surrounding Paris.

video:video.video:title: Child among several Palestinians killed by Israeli fire and bombings in Gaza
video:video.video:description: At least nine Palestinians were killed by Israeli fire and airstrikes in the war-torn Gaza Strip, health authorities said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Giant beehive unveiled in London for Winnie the Pooh's centenary
video:video.video:description: The 100th anniversary of Winnie the Pooh is being marked with a giant beehive installation in London that explores the relationship between bees, honey and the stories of the honey-loving bear.

video:video.video:title: California leads 12-state bid to freeze Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery deal
video:video.video:description: Twelve states challenged Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery on Monday, filing a lawsuit that argues the mega merger would “extinguish competition” in Hollywood and threaten jobs across the industry.

video:video.video:title: Darline Graham Nordone, sister of Lindsey Graham, picked to serve remainder of US Senate term
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, has been named as her late brother's temporary replacement in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans will return to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey': A character guide in 60 seconds
video:video.video:description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" premieres July 17. Here's a quick guide to the main characters from the nearly 3,000-year-old epic before you head to the theater.

video:video.video:title: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, will serve out remainder of his Senate term
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, has been named as her late brother's temporary replacement in the U.S. Senate.

video:video.video:title: Senators deliver emotional tributes to Lindsey Graham after his sudden death
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham’s desk was covered in black cloth and a vase of white roses as the Senate opened Monday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: In a sweet discovery, astronomers find sugar lurking in the space between stars
video:video.video:description: The space between stars in the Milky Way just got a little sweeter for Spanish researchers who've found evidence of a sugar which is found in raspberries and self tanning lotions.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran war moving ‘very fast,’ US will seek control of Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the war with Iran is moving “very fast” as U.S. forces pressed ahead with strikes targeting Tehran’s military capabilities, adding that Washington would seek to control the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Protesters hold walking vigil after ICE fatally shoots motorist in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a motorist in Maine on Monday. Dozens of demonstrators protesting ICE and President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown held a walking vigil in Biddeford that evening.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters deployed across Paris region forest to fight exceptional fire
video:video.video:description: It was among several wildfires in western Europe as the region bakes under its third red-alert heat wave this year.

video:video.video:title: Spanish PM flies over scene of deadly wildfire in Almeria
video:video.video:description: Spain is experiencing extreme heat, which combined with wind and little rainfall is creating the ideal conditions for small wildfires to grow unchecked.

video:video.video:title: Workers at Ebola center in Congo strike over unpaid salaries
video:video.video:description: Dozens of people working at an Ebola treatment center in the Rwampara general hospital in northeast Democratic Republic of Congo went on strike Monday over unpaid salaries and bonuses, shutting down the hospital, blocking the road leading to the medical facility and burning tires in front of the mai

video:video.video:title: US attacks Iran as Tehran retaliates against UAE tankers in Strait of Hormuz and Bahrain
video:video.video:description: The U.S. launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday morning, hours after President Donald Trump said Washington is “reinstating” a blockade on Iran in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Grandmothers in Kenya ask for a soccer World Cup to compete in
video:video.video:description: As the football world’s attention is caught by the World Cup, there is a group of unlikely fans from rural Kenya who want to prove that the elegant sport belongs to all generations.

video:video.video:title: Kerry Washington on reviving 'The Whoopi Monologues'
video:video.video:description: Kerry Washington stars in and produces a revival of Whoopi Goldberg's 1984 Broadway show "The Whoopi Monologues.” (July 13)

video:video.video:title: Reese Witherspoon reflects on 25th anniversary of 'Legally Blonde'
video:video.video:description: On the 25th anniversary of "Legally Blonde," Reese Witherspoon says playing "ultimate underdog" Elle Woods "changed my life."

video:video.video:title: Whoopi Goldberg reflects on 'The Whoopi Monologues'
video:video.video:description: As "The Whoopi Monologues" gets an Off-Broadway revival featuring Kerry Washington, Whoopi Goldberg looks back at her inspirations. (July 13)

video:video.video:title: Anya Taylor-Joy's World Cup 'stress'
video:video.video:description: Anya Taylor-Joy says the World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina has brought "a lot of stress" to her household because of her ties to both countries.

video:video.video:title: Oslo street's transformed into colourful gallery of graffiti art
video:video.video:description: Walls in one of Oslo's former industrial districts has become a giant canvas for street artists from around the world.

video:video.video:title: Man held over murder of wife and daughters in UK appears in South Africa court
video:video.video:description: Triple murder suspect Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, wanted by British authorities on suspicion of killing his wife and two young daughters in England, appeared in a Johannesburg court on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Chicago area zoo helps animals cool off amid heatwave
video:video.video:description: Chicago area zookeepers put out ice treats and produce for bears and other animals in the midst of a summer heatwave. (AP video: Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: Trump says FBI 'wasting their time' on probing Sen. Graham's death
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the FBI is “wasting their time” if it is investigating the death of Sen.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for July 14th: President Trump reverses course on Strait of Hormuz toll; Supreme Court justices testify before Congress; Israel vows harsh response to future attacks; Winnie The Pooh anniversary celebrated in London.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Mahmoud Khalil files suit alleging a 'public-private' conspiracy to target Israel's critics
video:video.video:description: Khalil was joined by his lawyers, fellow advocates and supporters at a press briefing in New York Tuesday where they took turns at the podium to discuss the lawsuit and their ongoing fight to continue speaking up for Palestinian rights.

video:video.video:title: Iraqi PM arrives for White House meeting with Trump
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump welcomed Iraq's new prime minister to the White House. Ali al-Zaidi's visit Tuesday aims to strengthen economic ties and attract investment.

video:video.video:title: Darline Graham, sister of late Sen. Lindsey Graham, has been sworn in to finish his term
video:video.video:description: Darline Graham, the sister of the late South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham, was sworn in by the Senate Tuesday, filling the seat just three days after her brother’s sudden death.

video:video.video:title: Hikes, helicopters and poisonous plants: behind-the-scenes on 'The Odyssey'
video:video.video:description: Zendaya and John Leguizamo chat about helicopter rides to Christopher Nolan's epic sets on "The Odyssey," and which plants to avoid.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's replacing the Strait of Hormuz fees with Gulf investments
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday backtracked on plans to charge ships for using the Strait of Hormuz, saying Gulf countries would instead invest in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Maine demand answers after ICE shoots Colombian man
video:video.video:description: Immigrant rights groups are demanding answers and planning more protests Tuesday over an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer's fatal shooting of a Maine driver.

video:video.video:title: Widespread flooding as heavy storms hit Russia’s Ural
video:video.video:description: Torrential rain and thunderstorms have gripped the region since the beginning of summer, and forecasters say the same weather is expected to continue in the coming days.

video:video.video:title: Colourful murals are taking over the streets of Oslo.
video:video.video:description: Founded in Germany in 2002, the 'Meeting of Styles' events has grown into a global non-profit network staging street art festivals worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Manta tracking project aims to protect Japan's ocean and coastal rays
video:video.video:description: Okinawa is one of Japan’s best-known manta ray habitats, but researchers say basic questions remain about how the animals move, where they feed and how they may be affected by warming seas, coral reef degradation and the pressure of tourism.

video:video.video:title: Warsh says Fed has 'no tolerance' for high inflation but provides no hints on next move
video:video.video:description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh pledged to make high inflation “a thing of the past” in his first congressional testimony Tuesday, yet provided no signal about the central bank’s next steps.

video:video.video:title: Iran says initial deal with US in state of 'crisis' and blames Washington
video:video.video:description: Iran said on Monday that an initial peace deal reached with the United States last month is in a state of "crisis" as Iran's military released footage it said showed missiles being launched earlier in the day in response to what it described as "US aggressions".

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans will return to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices rise following the latest fighting in the Middle East, as AI stocks sink
video:video.video:description: Oil prices jumped Monday following a weekend of attacks in the Middle East, while more losses for computer chip companies and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom weighed on stock markets.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, July 13th: Hospital workers in Ebola-stricken Congo strike over wages; Zelenskyy awards Macron the Order of Freedom; The UN condemns continued military confrontation between Iran and the US; Spanish researchers discover sugar in space.

video:video.video:title: Pomp and tradition meets conservation in annual swan count on the river Thames
video:video.video:description: The annual count of swans belonging to King Charles III has begun on the river Thames. The five day event is known as swan upping and dates back to the 12th Century.

video:video.video:title: Witness heard man say “I tried to stop” after ICE shooting in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a motorist in Maine on Monday, the second time in a week that U.S.

video:video.video:title: Feds hand over evidence in Renee Good and Alex Pretti killings to Minnesota prosecutors
video:video.video:description: Minnesota prosecutors announced Monday that they have obtained key evidence in their ongoing investigations into fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti during pitched protests against a federal immigration enforcement crackdown in the state earlier this year.

video:video.video:title: Racist comments targeting France’s World Cup team spark backlash
video:video.video:description: Comments questioning the nationality of France’s players have drawn backlash from French officials and others, reigniting a debate over racism and representation in soccer.

video:video.video:title: Security video shows deadly encounter
video:video.video:description: Footage from a security camera captured the shooting Monday during an immigration enforcement operation in the city of Biddeford.

video:video.video:title: Trump on the death of Lindsey Graham
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump spoke about the late South Carolina U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham

video:video.video:title: 'Gilded Age' actors show support for Palestinian rights activist Mahmoud Khalil amid lawsuit filing
video:video.video:description: Khalil was joined Tuesday by his lawyers, fellow advocates and supporters, including 'The Gilded Age' actors Morgan Spector and Cynthia Nixon, at a press briefing in New York where they discussed the lawsuit and the ongoing fight for Palestinian rights.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 14
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, July 14th: Protests in Maine following ICE shooting; Iran retaliates for latest U.S. airstrikes; Houthi rebels in Yemen blame Saudi Arabia for attack on airport; Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to temporarily replace him in Senate.

video:video.video:title: Video shows flooded streets in northwest Louisiana
video:video.video:description: Video from Bossier City, Louisiana, shows vehicles driving through flooded streets, with some getting stuck.

video:video.video:title: How to choose the best backup power when the lights go out
video:video.video:description: From portable battery banks and generators to larger home systems, there are many backup power choices to consider as extreme weather ramps up and outages happen more often and last longer.

video:video.video:title: Grandmothers in Kenya ask for a soccer World Cup to compete in
video:video.video:description: As the soccer world’s attention is caught by the World Cup, a group of unlikely fans from rural Kenya want to prove that the sport belongs to all generations.

video:video.video:title: Morning shots from scene of Bangkok pub fire that killed at least 27
video:video.video:description: Most of the people who were killed in a huge fire in a Bangkok music bar were found trapped in windowless bathrooms where they may have sought to escape the flames that claimed at least 27 lives, authorities said Monday as investigations began.

video:video.video:title: Massive AI buildout drives up costs for laptops and electricity
video:video.video:description: American consumers are being hit with another high-cost headache. The surge of investment in data centers to power artificial intelligence has made memory chips, computer processors and other equipment, as well as electricity, more expensive.

video:video.video:title: Senators deliver emotional tributes to Lindsey Graham after his sudden death
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans returned to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Several found dead in lift after Brussels building fire
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Tuesday at a building being renovated in downtown Brussels, injuring two people and leaving six missing.

video:video.video:title: Tehran reaction to US threats over Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Tehran residents on Tuesday showed mixed reactions to the United States decision to reimpose a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz right before U.S.

video:video.video:title: Community leaders, organizers rally to demand accountability after ICE shooting in Maine
video:video.video:description: Community leaders and organizers rallied to demand accountability after an ICE agent fatally shot a motorist in Biddeford, Maine on Monday.

video:video.video:title: US-Russian crew blasts off for stint on space station
video:video.video:description: A U.S.-Russian crew successfully blasted off on a mission to the International Space Station on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Community mourns at makeshift memorial for man fatally shot by ICE officer in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal officer shot and killed a driver during an immigration enforcement operation in Biddeford, Maine.

video:video.video:title: $50 million paid for huge T-Rex fossil at auction
video:video.video:description: A Tyrannosaurus rex fossil billed as one of the world’s largest and most complete specimens has sold for a record $50.1 million at an auction.

video:video.video:title: Trump reduces size of two national monuments in Utah
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Monday sharply reduced the size of two national monuments in Utah, undoing protections established by his Democratic predecessors on public lands that are sacred among many Native Americans.

video:video.video:title: Cuba suffers third outage of national power grid in less than two weeks
video:video.video:description: Cuba experienced another outage in the National Energy System (SEN) on Tuesday, leaving the entire country without power, according to the Ministry of Energy and Mines.

video:video.video:title: Bastille Day in Paris puts European military might centre stage
video:video.video:description: It acknowledges the day Parisians stormed the Bastille fortress and prison in 1789, helping spark the French Revolution that overthrew the monarchy.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails 'tremendous chemistry' with new Iraqi prime minister during White House visit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump welcomed Iraq’s new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi to the White House Tuesday, hailing their "tremendous chemistry" and touting closer U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Reporter shows scene of ICE shooting in Maine
video:video.video:description: The shooting of a Colombian man in Maine by ICE agents happened one block from his apartment building. AP's Patrick Whittle shows the scene. (AP Video: Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: Tai Chi practitioners seek balance in fast-paced Beijing
video:video.video:description: Widely practiced, Tai Chi is still a popular way for people to escape the hustle and bustle of city life in Beijing and unwind.

video:video.video:title: Extreme pogo is turning pogo sticks into a serious sport
video:video.video:description: Extreme pogo athletes train for years and do flips, spins and gravity-defying tricks to compete at the highest level — even though there’s no prize money on the line.

video:video.video:title: Scientists collect data behind forecasts at UK weather station, as country swelters in latest heatwave
video:video.video:description: Weather stations turn heat, wind, rain and sunshine into the numbers behind forecasts, records and climate reports.

video:video.video:title: Multiple blasts as Yemen airport hit in airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Multiple blasts were seen at Sanaa airport in Yemen on Monday in what Iran-backed Houthi rebels said were Saudi airstrikes while the internationally recognized government in Yemen said they were meant to prevent an Iranian plane from landing.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya says working on 'The Odyssey' was 'Such an honor'
video:video.video:description: Zendaya says she was “the new kid on the block” while filming “The Odyssey,” but the cast and crew made her feel like part of the family. (July 14)

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans without tickets get to watch the semifinal from the tribunes thanks to a virtual reality experience
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans without World Cup tickets can pretend to watch the semifinal game between France and Spain from the tribunes at an Atlanta bar for cheaper.

video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 1 missing after pontoon boat fire near Alcatraz Island off San Francisco
video:video.video:description: San Francisco Fire Chief Dean Crispen said one person died and another was missing after a fire on a pontoon boat off San Francisco.

video:video.video:title: Iran's president condems Trump's 'rude language' against his country
video:video.video:description: Iran's president on Tuesday rejected U.S. President Donald's Trump "rude language" against his country as the U.S. launches further strikes in the region.

video:video.video:title: US carries out out new wave of strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. carried out another wave of strikes on Iran as it reimposed a blockade, striking dozens of targets over seven hours, the U.S. military's Central Command said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Search underway after boat sinks near Alcatraz Island, leaving 1 dead, 2 missing
video:video.video:description: One person died, two people were missing and 16 others were rescued from the waters off San Francisco after a pontoon boat carrying 19 people sank Tuesday afternoon, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Smoke from Minnesota wildfires triggers air quality alert
video:video.video:description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week, exposing millions of people to dangerous air pollution.

video:video.video:title: Philly youth soccer clubs hope that World Cup excitement leads to lasting support
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia youth soccer players got an opportunity to experience the World Cup up close. Now families and coaches hope the tournament's excitement brings lasting support for their under-resourced neighborhoods.

video:video.video:title: Grieving relatives and friends of Bangkok fire victims express sorrow and anger
video:video.video:description: Relatives and friends of the victims of a huge fire at a Bangkok music bar arrived at the Institute of Forensic Medicine on Tuesday to receive the bodies of the deceased.

video:video.video:title: Boston welcomes traffic cone gift in celebration of Scottish camaraderie following World Cup pranks
video:video.video:description: The cone was welcomed as the latest chapter in Boston’s unlikely love affair with Scotland’s Tartan Army, whose habit of placing traffic cones atop statues during last month’s World Cup turned the cone into a symbol of the tournament’s spirit.

video:video.video:title: Security video shows deadly encounter with ICE in Biddeford, Maine
video:video.video:description: Footage from a security camera captured Monday's shooting during an immigration enforcement operation in the city of Biddeford, Maine.

video:video.video:title: Moment when House passed the bill to make daylight saving time permanent
video:video.video:description: There will be no turning back the clock if the House has its way. The House passed a bill Tuesday that would make daylight saving time permanent.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate in Madrid as Spain shuts down France 2-0 to earn spot in World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Spanish fans celebrated in Madrid following Spain's 2-0 victory over France on Tuesday. In footage captured by the Associated Press, Spain fans could be seen taking to the streets of Madrid following the win, and later being dispersed by police.

video:video.video:title: Rita Ora talks playing Queen of Hearts in the 'Descendants' franchise
video:video.video:description: Rita Ora explains how her Queen of Hearts has depth, as she reprises the role for "Descendants: Wicked Wonderland." (July 15)

video:video.video:title: US reimposes blockade on Iran after Tehran's attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The US military early Wednesday reimposed a blockade on Iranian ports over Tehran’s attacks on ships trying to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, sparking new strikes on nations hosting US forces as an interim deal to end the war further unraveled.

video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 3 missing after boat carrying mostly family members sinks near Alcatraz Island
video:video.video:description: One person died, three people were missing and 16 were rescued from the waters off San Francisco after a boat sank Tuesday afternoon while carrying mostly family members as part of a memorial service, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Spain and Gibraltar remove border fence in a historic EU-UK treaty
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people who travel every day between the southern tip of Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar will no longer have to cross a physical border beginning Wednesday after a border fence was fully removed, allowing a new freedom of movement under a historic European Union-U.K. treaty
